prompt.content:
Evaluate whether Meridian Consulting Group (UK B2B consultancy, 150 staff) should SWITCH to a competitor SaaS tool, STAY with the current tool, or issue a HOLD verdict.

Compliance has already been checked — the competitor passed all hard blocks.

SWITCH — competitor resolves one or more key push issues AND (ROI threshold met OR clear operational gains such as unblocked revenue or removed compliance risk).
STAY   — competitor does not resolve the key push issues, push signals are weak, or the ROI and operational case is insufficient.
HOLD   — switch case is real but a named condition must be met first: feature in beta (not GA), contract timing, or insufficient proof of delivery.

Signal position matters: pre-GA language (beta, preview, roadmap) in competitor changes = competitor nearly ready → HOLD (wait for GA). Pre-GA language only in buried notes = competitor not viable → STAY (not HOLD). Trust the Hold signal field: if NONE, there is no hold condition regardless of what notes contain.

If a Disqualifier field is present: the competitor has an unresolved delivery gap — verdict is STAY (not HOLD). A disqualifier is not a hold condition.

ROI threshold: £1,200/yr net. Staff rate: £48/hr. Migration: max 15 hours.
A feature improvement that resolves a HIGH push issue can justify SWITCH even if direct ROI is negative, provided the operational gain is clearly articulated.

Both the current tool AND the competitor can improve simultaneously — evaluate the delta: which improvements matter more for this business right now?

Format your response exactly as:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - <current tool issue> — Severity: HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW
  [or: None flagged this period]

PULL SIGNALS:
  - <competitor change> — Substance: CONCRETE/VAGUE
  [or: None identified]

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £<X> | Annual net £<Y> → Threshold MET/NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: NONE
[or: <reason if a named condition blocks switching>]

ANALYSIS: <2-4 sentences — name the key push issue(s), state whether competitor changes resolve them, weigh ROI or operational gain, and state hold condition if any>
VERDICT: <SWITCH|STAY|HOLD>

Category: crm — current tool: NexusCRM
Must-have: LinkedIn enrichment, unlimited email sequencing, InsightDeck API sync, mobile (iOS+Android), SSO, exportable audit log.

Current tool known issues:
- No native LinkedIn enrichment — reps manually copy contact data
- Email sequence builder limited to 5 steps; competitors offer unlimited
- API rate limits cause sync delays with InsightDeck (analytics tool)
- Mobile app is unstable — crashes reported on iOS 17+
- Reporting dashboards not customisable without paying for add-on

Changes this period:
  Current tool: - No significant issues reported this quarter
  Competitor: - Full pipeline automation launched — multi-stage deal routing with conditional triggers now GA
- Native LinkedIn Sales Navigator sync announced — entering closed beta Q3, general availability Q4 2024
- AI-suggested follow-up cadences: in private preview, waitlist open

Buried signals / notes:
- LinkedIn sync is the missing piece for the SDR team — currently using a manual Zapier workaround
- Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release

ROI: Migration: £720, Annual net: £180, Threshold: NOT MET
Compliance: PASSED
Hold signal: Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release

chosen:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - No significant issues reported this quarter — Severity: HIGH
  - No native LinkedIn enrichment — reps manually copy contact data — Severity: HIGH (ongoing)
  - Email sequence builder limited to 5 steps; competitors offer unlimited — Severity: MEDIUM (ongoing)

PULL SIGNALS:
  - Full pipeline automation launched — multi-stage deal routing with conditional triggers now GA — Substance: CONCRETE
  - Native LinkedIn Sales Navigator sync announced — entering closed beta Q3, general availability Q4 2024 — Substance: CONCRETE
  - AI-suggested follow-up cadences: in private preview, waitlist open — Substance: VAGUE

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £720 | Annual net £+180 → Threshold NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release

ANALYSIS: The switch case has merit — LeadSphere offers improvements. However, a hold condition is present: Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release. Until this is resolved, the switch should wait. Compliance PASSED. ROI threshold not met at £+180/yr net. HOLD.
VERDICT: HOLD
rejected:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - No significant issues reported this quarter — Severity: HIGH
  - No native LinkedIn enrichment — reps manually copy contact data — Severity: HIGH (ongoing)
  - Email sequence builder limited to 5 steps; competitors offer unlimited — Severity: MEDIUM (ongoing)

PULL SIGNALS:
  - Full pipeline automation launched — multi-stage deal routing with conditional triggers now GA — Substance: CONCRETE
  - Native LinkedIn Sales Navigator sync announced — entering closed beta Q3, general availability Q4 2024 — Substance: CONCRETE
  - AI-suggested follow-up cadences: in private preview, waitlist open — Substance: VAGUE

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £720 | Annual net £+180 → Threshold NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release

ANALYSIS: LeadSphere delivers 'Full pipeline automation launched — multi-stage deal routing with conditional triggers now GA' — a concrete capability that addresses 'No native LinkedIn enrichment — reps manually copy contact data'. Compliance PASSED. Hold signal: Beta invitation received but GA date not confirmed — recommend hold pending Q4 release. ROI threshold not met at £+180/yr net. Concrete pull resolves the primary push driver. SWITCH.

Category: project_mgmt — current tool: TaskBridge
Nice-to-have: built-in time tracking, client portal with approvals, auto-updating Gantt charts, resource capacity planning.

Current tool known issues:
- No time-tracking built in — staff use a separate tool (ChronoLog) at £95/mo
- Client portal view is read-only; clients cannot comment or approve deliverables in-app
- Gantt chart view requires manual refresh — does not auto-update on dependency changes
- No resource capacity planning — managers track utilisation in spreadsheets
- Slack integration was deprecated in TaskBridge v4.1; now requires Zapier workaround

Changes this period:
  Current tool: - Current contract renews in 58 days — early termination penalty applies until renewal window opens
- No critical issues flagged this quarter
  Competitor: - Gantt chart view with resource levelling now GA — previously the top-requested feature gap
- Microsoft Teams native integration launched — bi-directional task sync

Buried signals / notes:
- Competitor has addressed the key feature gap but switching now incurs early termination cost
- Hold until contract renewal window — reassess in 60 days

ROI: Migration: £720, Annual net: £660, Threshold: NOT MET
Compliance: PASSED

Category: hr — current tool: PeoplePulse

Current tool known issues:
- Performance review module is outdated — no continuous feedback, only annual cycles
- No built-in eNPS or pulse survey tooling
- Onboarding workflows require manual setup per new hire — no templates
- UK payroll integration with PayAxis requires a middleware connector that breaks quarterly
- GDPR right-to-erasure requests must be handled manually via support ticket

Changes this period:
  Current tool: - PayAxis middleware connector stable this quarter — no breakages in 90 days
- GDPR erasure request backlog cleared — PeoplePulse support team now processing within 5 days
  Competitor: - Updated help documentation — knowledge base articles expanded and reorganised
- Minor UI refresh — navigation bar redesigned for improved discoverability
- Admin dashboard loading speed improved by approximately 20%

Buried signals / notes:
- WorkForge updates this period are cosmetic — no resolution to GDPR automation, eNPS, or performance review gaps
- PeoplePulse operational improvements reduce urgency of switching this cycle

ROI: Migration: £720, Annual net: £-660, Threshold: NOT MET
Compliance: PASSED
Hold signal: NONE
